text: The glow of the purple room intrigues me, its emptiness makes the atmosphere both inviting and strangely alienating. As I move closer, the stark lines and reflections on the glass make me conscious of my own presence. When I look at the R+D wall, the immersive lights and projected text speak directly to my unease—what is hidden, what is revealed? I feel exposed, and at the same time, I sense a curious empowerment—like a thought freshly formed, flickering under scrutiny. This place asks me to question what it means to observe, to be observed, and to exist at the boundary of illumination and shadow.
